Once-only attestations. Mandatory for WUAs. Fully mitigates RP linkability. Highest issuance overhead.
Limited-time. Multiple uses, periodic re-issuance. Mandatory minimum (A or B) for all PIDs/attestations. Partial linkability mitigation.
Rotating batch. Issued in batches; used in random order; resets when exhausted. Optional. Partial mitigation.
Per-RP. Same RP always gets the same attestation, but different RPs get different ones. Optional. Full intra-RP unlinkability.
A or B. Method A is always mandatory for WUAs specifically.

PID mdoc attestation type?: Where do RPs live in the trust topology?
eu.europa.ec.eudi.pid.1: No list for RPs. Each RP gets access certificates from an Access CA; Wallet Units verify those.
Must live in WSCA/WSCD (e.g. PID keys, WUA keys, high-security attestation keys). Non-critical assets may live in a keystore.

PID mdoc attestation type?: Can the Wallet Provider see what's in the Wallet Unit?
eu.europa.ec.eudi.pid.1: No. WIAM_12a explicitly forbids it (attestations present, status, attribute values, transaction log all hidden).

PID mdoc attestation type?: Does the Wallet Unit carry a registration certificate?
eu.europa.ec.eudi.pid.1: No. Registration certificates are for PID/Attestation Providers and RPs. Wallet Units carry WUAs (and post-Topic-C: WIAs + KAs).
Within 24 hours, via a communication channel independent of the Wallet Unit (WURevocation_14, _16).
